---
title: 'Magnets. Wicker Furniture. Mending the Soul.'
date: '2020-02-21T15:42:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: false
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '5452756977329584855'
blogger_filename: '/2020/02/magnets-wicker-furniture-mending-soul.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Musings'
---

<div>I have these two magnets. They look like shiney rocks. When held a certain way they snap together, making a high pitch clicking sound.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>When you hold one firmly and turn the second 180*, they push each other apart instead. Try as you might, you cannot push them together and make them stick unless you turn them the right way.</div><div><br></div><div>So it is with our souls.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>The principle has many applications. In each instance, things that try to come together will push apart until they are turned the right way.</div><div><br></div><div>When Flavia and I spent 13 years never quite being able to come together, it was me who needed to change, primarily. When my soul was turned the right way, we suddenly fit together perfectly. That took a miracle of God, but it was one I had to press into before getting. It wasn't easy.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>To be "wicked" isn't a simple insult or label. A wick is something that is twisted. Wicker furniture is material that had been twisted into it's present shape. When our soul is found to be wicked, that simply means that we were designed to function one way and our soul is twisted out of shape. They further out of order we are, the more twisted or soul is. The most evil person imaginable is 180* out of line, because once you got 181* you are turning back into right order.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Every instance of an aspect of life that doesn't line up with God's Word is an aspect of life that is twisted.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>When people attempt to satisfy their soul in a union of bodies (sex) outside of God's order, it fails to satisfy.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Even if you whole heartedly believe you were born different, you weren't. Your soul is simply twisted out of shape, once it realignes it will change. I spent years unable to want what God wants for me, until he realigned my soul. Then "suddey" I wanted what He wanted. In Truth, in wasn't sudden. It was a very long journey, hard fought small wins, and many tiny lessons before a form of victory started taking shape.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Each turn was God shifting my soul, by and inch at a time, into the right shape. The resistance I felt was from being out of shape, out of position. The closer I got to being in the right position, the less fight I felt in me against it.</div><div><br></div><div>The hard thing about this type of healing, is that there's no "cure". No six steps. No magic pill or formula. You can't take a class and suddenly find your soul craving the right things.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>It's a journey, a work of God. You either start that journey and let Him work in you to mend your soul; or, you continue to twist further out of shape.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>If you have aspects of your life where you desire things God says are harmful for you... Your soul is twisted in those areas. You cannot untwist yourself. You cannot "choose" to be untwisted. Neither, though, is your will (choice) a non factor.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>The key is to surrender to God's Presence.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>You cannot choose to change:&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>You cannot try harder a better knowledge of good, that's the wrong tree, the Tree of Knowledge. Trying harder to "be good" is a religious activity for Pharisees. It's fruitless, and it's frustrating.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>You can choose to enter His Presence:&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Instead, your goal is to choose the Tree of Life. In practical terms, that means getting into God's presence as often as possible, as many ways as possible.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Be mindful and aware of His presence. Pray. Read your Bible. Read good books by thought leaders. Listen to teachings and Podcasts. Attend meetings and conferences. Meet with friends and talk about God.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>All of these, in the Wrong Mindset, could be fruitless religious activities. Dead works.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>All of these, in the Right Mindset, can be life giving fruitful activities. Plugging into Life.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Try saying: "God, Daddy, in this moment I submit myself to you. Have your way with my soul today. How would you like to spend the next X minutes?"</div><div><br></div><div>Don't expect one session to change you in noticable ways, any more than ten seconds in an oven makes cookie dough into a cookie. But enough ten seconds stacked onto each other makes a cookie.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Spend time with your Daddy today.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>#Selah</div><div><br></div><div>Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1582299741867153-0-8a29439f29e5.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0"   src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1582299741867153-0-8a29439f29e5.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1582299737946671-1-2ebfdec8ee07.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0"   src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1582299737946671-1-2ebfdec8ee07.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><br></div>
